subject: President Trump helps restore Project Safe Neighborhoods
body:
Last Friday, President Donald J. Trump traveled to Kansas City, Missouri, to speak at the Project Safe Neighborhoods Conference. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d8af14ed6d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • December 10, 2018
---
| | |
# **President Trump helps restore Project Safe Neighborhoods**

**Last Friday,** President Donald J. Trump traveled to Kansas City, Missouri, to speak at the Project Safe Neighborhoods Conference. The goal of the visit was simple: to show appreciation for our great law enforcement officials and help revive a program with a record of effective crime prevention in American communities.

**“Everyone here today** is committed to the same vital goal: liberating our communities from crime and securing the right of all citizens to live in safety and to live in peace,” the President said to an audience of men and women in law enforcement.

**Restoring** **respect for our law enforcement is essential.** “For too long, many politicians and radical activists have been smearing and slandering our police, making life easier for criminals and harder for law-abiding Americans,” the President explained. The results of this renewed commitment have begun to show: This year, an all-time record has been set for prosecution of violent crimes. And in a remarkable turnaround, murder rates in America’s largest cities are projected to drop by more than 8 percent compared to 2016.

**“You keep America safe.** And you maybe don’t hear it enough, or sometimes don’t feel it enough: You do an incredible job,” President Trump said. “We stand with you 100 percent.”

[See the President’s full remarks at the 2018 Project Safe Neighborhoods National Conference](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=92769d1e8f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump attends the 2018 Army-Navy Game**

**The Commander in Chief presided over a coin flip between historic rivals** at this year’s Army-Navy football game in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.

**Flanked** **by Secretary of Defense James Mattis** , President Trump flipped the coin in favor of the Navy football team—but Army ultimately took the win. The Army and Navy football teams have faced off on the gridiron nearly every year since 1890, and with President Trump’s appearance over the weekend, he becomes the 10th Commander in Chief to attend the game.

**“It was my honor** to attend today’s #ArmyNavyGame in Philadelphia. A GREAT game played all around by our HEROES. Congratulations @ArmyWP_Football on the win!” the President tweeted.

[Watch: President Trump attends this year’s Army-Navy Game](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5f3ecb137d&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Gov. Mike Huckabee
subject: Come Cruise with us!
body:
| | This will be an unforgettable trip.
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FECXgvVBAH18GBWRCy8D9S_H0X-LMKxuVQZOVdM48FB6clUdS6lpIFIKWvxyXp9e2cOQvzJtyDDTF_AeDLkTIA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181211com01&n=1)
| Peter, I want to extend an invitation to you to join me on an unforgettable trip to the Baltics in 2019 from August 9 to August 17! After many years of the Israel Experiences and our most recent trip the Mediterranean Experience cruise, we are bringing you another phenomenal cruise, the Baltic Experience! This all-inclusive cruise will feature some of the most beautiful places in the Baltic Sea, and we will get to travel to and absorb these destinations aboard a luxury cruise ship from the Azamara Cruise line. Together we will visit Stockholm and Visby Sweden, Talinn Estonia, St. Petersburg Russia, Helsinki Finland, and Copenhagen Denmark! If you have traveled with us before, you already know that all-inclusive means exactly that and there are no surprises along the way. The Baltic Experience will be no different. And when the weather is often unbearably hot throughout most of the US in August, we will be enjoying the cool and delightful weather that makes travel in the Baltic region perfect. Once again we have the entire ship and will fill our time on board with spectacular entertainment and special guests. In addition to the cruise we have multiple pre and post cruise offerings you can choose from to extend your trip. Our last cruise sold out in record time, so we encourage you to get your cabin secured soon if you are able to join us. You can learn more about the exciting trip including all the cruise details and rate information on [The Greatest Trip's website](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FECXgvVBAH18GBWRCy8D9S_H0X-LMKxuVQZOVdM48FB6clUdS6lpIFIKWvxyXp9e2cOQvzJtyDDTF_AeDLkTIA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181211com01&n=2). If you have additional questions about the trip after visiting the website, [contact The Baltic Experience team](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FECXgvVBAH18GBWRCy8D9S_H0X-LMKxuVQZOVdM48FBgEtmL7MM07_M1G-_cBmCG&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181211com01&n=3). [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FECXgvVBAH18GBWRCy8D9S_H0X-LMKxuVQZOVdM48FB6clUdS6lpIFIKWvxyXp9e2cOQvzJtyDDTF_AeDLkTIA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181211com01&n=4) I just wanted to share this opportunity with you. We would love to see you on board! God Bless,

subject: Job Openings, Prison Reform, and Surging American Factories
body:
“There were 1 million more jobs than unemployed workers in the most recent government count of job openings,” Jeff Cox reports for CNBC. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=15ce9be87e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Job Openings Back Over 7 Million and Near a Record High**

“There were 1 million more jobs than unemployed workers in the most recent government count of job openings,” Jeff Cox reports for CNBC.

“Total available jobs stood at 7.08 million for October, the second-highest level surpassed only by the 7.3 million in August, the Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey showed. By comparison, there were just 6.08 million Americans classified as unemployed for the month.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=96af95794f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “President Trump has courageously thrown his weight behind a reform bill that will dramatically improve how we rehabilitate criminals and help keep them out of overcrowded prisons. Joining him are a majority in the Senate as well as numerous groups representing law enforcers and prosecutors,” John Koufos writes in the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dd54a3df55&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| In _[Bloomberg](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b40f77ed4f&e=3ee4985eea)_ , Alexandre Tanzi reports that openings in U.S. factories are surging like never before. “The job openings rate for the durable-goods manufacturing industry reached 4 percent in October, a record in data back to 2000, according to figures issued Monday by the Labor Department. That’s up from 3.7 percent in September and 3.1 percent a year earlier.”
---
|
---
| “A little over a year ago, President Donald Trump promised he would recognize Jerusalem as the capital of Israel and, six months later, he delivered on that promise by officially moving the U.S. embassy from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem,” Alex Titus writes in _[The Hill](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=40e1136ddb&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Standing up for Israel at the United Nations, confronting Iran, and empowering the Jewish state to defend its interests have been successful outcomes of [the Trump Administration’s] policy agenda.”
---
|
---
| “Ronald Reagan understood it is only from strength that we may find a true peace,” [White House Director of Trade and Manufacturing Policy Peter Navarro](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=569eb90368&e=3ee4985eea) writes in _RealClearPolitics_. President Trump has embraced that banner and expanded it for more complex times with a key addendum: “Economic security is national security.”
---
|

subject: Robert Mueller
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|

| | President Trump continues to call Robert Mueller's investigation into his campaign's ties to Russia a "witch hunt." Here's the thing, Peter: witch hunts involve _false_ allegations. **Mueller's investigation has already yielded indictments, confessions, or convictions from 33 people and three companies -- that we know of.** They've released over 300 pages of documentation through court filings and more are coming out all the time. Just last week, Mueller brought to light that Trump's campaign chief, Paul Manafort, lied to prosecutors. Five times. This is no witch hunt, it's a hunt for the truth. The truth that the American people deserve to know. Mueller, the Justice Department, and the FBI are doing a fair and thorough job and they should be allowed to complete their work without political interference. [**It's time for Congress to step in and protect Robert Mueller's investigation. Sign on if you agree.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffdb1/4462dc26/89fe05ab/201efb2f/4234249764/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181211m") With gratitude, Sherrod

name: Team Ryan
subject: Fwd: How have the General Motors layoffs affected you?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress
body:
We've been hearing stories about how the GM layoffs are impacting communities across Ohio and across the country. | |
---
We've been hearing stories about how the GM layoffs are impacting communities across Ohio and across the country. We've included a few below. [If you're willing, please click here to share your own story with us:](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf5944/747721752/VEsH/ "http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+If+youre+willing+pleasenbspclick+here+to&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") [SUBMIT YOUR STORY](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf5947/747721752/VEsE/ "https://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=2+-+SUBMIT+YOUR+STORY&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") _Disclaimer: We will only share your first name, city, and story. _ "The impact of the GM layoffs and possible plant closure have had a visceral effect on me. I grew up in Warren and have only known GM to be a part of the community. This feels like abandonment at a basic level. I understand, intellectually that this is "not personal, it's only business," but all of us in the Valley treated GM like it was our "baby." We knew that we should buy American cars, GM cars, because if we didn't, our neighbor or parent, or relative could lose their job if we didn't." **_\--Jacci, Youngstown, OH_** "Dear Representative Ryan, I do not have a personal story to share, but I do want to thank you for advocating for the hard-working men and women at the Lordstown plant who are being victimized by GM. That corporations such as GM would benefit so dramatically from the recently adopted tax legislation and turn around and close plants, crushing communities, is so cynical as to be obscene. I hope the House of Representatives launches an ongoing investigation, following the money." **_\--Rolla, Columbus, OH_** "I know of 12 families, all of which work at Lordstown. Each is going through significant challenges. These are great paying jobs. The folks put in a great deal of effort at that plant. The loss hollows out more of the region and puts stress on communities all over the region. It begs the question, do we really have American companies who are beholden to the American worker? If not, then why does the company get to enjoy all the luxuries of living in America? Keep fighting the good fight." **_\--Amy, Hudson, OH_** [We want to make sure every person affected by the GM closings has their voice heard. Submit your story, or share this link with friends and family >>](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf5946/747721752/VEsF/ "http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=3+-+We+want+to+make+sure+every+person+affect&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") "My husband has worked in the GM assembly plant since 1995, and with overtime hours factored in, could retire in 5 years. If GM closes Lordstown, my husband will have to transfer to another GM facility, and chances are, that's going to mean his not being able to come home except on weekends, and maybe not even that often. I'm about to start treatment for breast cancer, and not having my husband here during this time will be extremely difficult." **_\--Beth, Warren, OH_** "I have a friend and co-workers father that works for the company. It is heartbreaking to see friends and total strangers lose their careers. Especially the breadwinners in the households. I understand because it happened to my family during the holiday season. It is hard to bounce back. If it were not for faith, family and friends helping I would have been homeless. President Barack Obama extended unemployment that kept my household afloat. The Democrats did everything they could to help me and families like mine. It never made the news that we were looking at our careers. Please inform Congress to do something to help these families. I am praying for a miracle to happen to restore this company. It will cause a ripple effect to all of the other businesses in these areas. What effect one layoff affects the entire country." _\--Amanda, Huntsville, AL_ "I've got 2 brothers-in-law currently working at the Lordstown plant, a third brother-in-law working as a supplier to Lordstown. My father-in-law retired after 40+ years of service. They've earned a good living, and for that, they're very appreciative. Our families have been loyal GM owners and supporters our entire lives. I myself have never had anything but a GM vehicle in my driveway and instilled the same beliefs in my children to own GM. But it's becoming very difficult for me to continue supporting a company that demonstrates such greed and disregard for their employees and the American people." **_\--Mike, Warren, OH_** "I have never owned or leased a non-GM car. I am in tears! Right now we have a Chevrolet Cruz and before that a Malibu. Just a day before the announcement that the Cruz plant would close, we received a card in the mail telling us about the features of the 2019 Cruz. Needless to say, I was shocked! I have always said that I would only buy an American car and now I am left with very few choices!" **_\--Maryann, Brooklyn, NY_** "As a lifelong resident of 54 years and a GM Lordstown employee for the past 24 years, GM is taking away the only source of income for my family. It's easy to tell someone to go to another facility but nobody seems to give a damn what distress that puts on the employee and their family. I have an 81-year-old recently widowed mother to take care of - how do I leave her when I am the only local family she has?" **_\--Gary, Columbiana, OH_** [If you've been affected by these layoffs or know someone who has, please share your story here.](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf5949/747721752/VEsC/ "http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=4+-+If+youve+been+affected+by+these+layoffs+&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") Tim is doing everything possible to get justice and support for laid-off workers, including calling for Congressional hearings on how GM used their massive tax cut. We will provide updates as we have them. In the meantime, thank you so much for sharing your stories with us -- and thank you for all you do, \-- Team Ryan _**PREVIOUS EMAIL**_ **Folks, I've been speaking out every way I possibly can about the General Motors layoffs that will hurt families in Ohio and across the country.** I've met with Vice President Mike Pence about a federal response. I will speak to GM CEO Mary Barra this week about building a different vehicle at the Lordstown plant, so workers can keep their jobs. I've called for Congressional hearings on how GM used the massive tax break they received last year. **But my most important mission is to make sure that GM, Congress, and the Trump Administration hear YOUR story. ** [Tell us how the GM layoffs are affecting you, your family, and your community. We will make sure your voice is heard >>](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf5948/747721752/VEsD/ "https://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=5+-+Tell+us+how+the+GM+layoffs+are+affecting&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") [SUBMIT YOUR STORY](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf594b/747721752/VEsA/ "https://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=6+-+SUBMIT+YOUR+STORY&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") _Disclaimer: We will only share your first name, city, and story. _ I remain heartbroken and furious about this situation, and the media has noticed: **WKBN:** _"Congressman Ryan has harsh words for GM after it pulls Chevy Cruze"_ **Washington Examiner:** "_Tim Ryan boils over in __floor_ _ speech after GM layoffs"_ **WKSU:** _"Rep. Tim Ryan Calls for Congressional Review of General Motors"_ GM Lordstown was an anchor for our community in Ohio's 13th District. I know across the country, these layoffs have effects that will echo far past one administration or one news cycle. I will fight tooth and nail to make sure the new Congress takes bold, immediate action to keep jobs in the United States. It's imperative that we generate more opportunities so workers and their families can earn a living wage, afford high-quality health care, send their kids to good schools, and retire with dignity. [Your voice deserves to be heard. If you're willing, please share your story about how the GM layoffs are affecting you and your community.](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf594a/747721752/VEsB/ "http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/s/submit-your-story-gm?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=7+-+Your+voice+deserves+to+be+heard+If+youre&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") Thank you so much, \-- Tim P.S. If you don't have a story to share but know someone who might, [share our form on Facebook so that everyone's voice can be heard.](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf594d/747721752/VEsO/ "https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Faction.timryanforcongress.com%2Fpage%2Fs%2Fsubmit-your-story-gm%3Fsource&subsource=fbshare&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=8+-+share+our+form+on+Facebook+so+that+every&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2")

* * *
Update Email Preferences [Receive Fewer Emails](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf594c/747721752/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.timryanforcongress.com/page/signup/fewer-emails?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=9+-+Receive+Fewer+Emails&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2") [ Unsubscribe](http://action.timryanforcongress.com/page/m/d7a90f2/5486deaa/25cbbdda/dcf594f/747721752/VEsHBQ/ "http://action.timryanforcongress.com/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=10+-++Unsubscribe&utm_campaign=em20181211_gmshare2&source=em20181211_gmshare2")

# Meet Tim Ryan
Tim Ryan is a champion for working families in Ohio's 13th District and is known for challenging both parties to do more to rebuild the middle class. Tim believes that every hardworking family deserves a good job, a quality education for their kids, affordable healthcare, and the security of knowing they can retire with dignity. Tim was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2002 and has been fighting for working people ever since.

I am in tears! Right now we have a Chevrolet Cruz and before that a Malibu. Just a day before the announcement that the Cruz plant would close, we received a card in the mail telling us about the features of the 2019 Cruz. Needless to say, I was shocked! I have always said that I would only buy an American car and now I am left with very few choices!" \--Maryann, Brooklyn, NY "As a lifelong resident of 54 years and a GM Lordstown employee for the past 24 years, GM is taking away the only source of income for my family. It's easy to tell someone to go to another facility but nobody seems to give a damn what distress that puts on the employee and their family. I have an 81-year-old recently widowed mother to take care of - how do I leave her when I am the only local family she has?" \--Gary, Columbiana, OH "" Tim is doing everything possible to get justice and support for laid-off workers, including calling for Congressional hearings on how GM used their massive tax cut. We will provide updates as we have them. In the meantime, thank you so much for sharing your stories with us -- and thank you for all you do, \-- Team Ryan PREVIOUS EMAIL Folks, I've been speaking out every way I possibly can about the General Motors layoffs that will hurt families in Ohio and across the country. I've met with Vice President Mike Pence about a federal response. I will speak to GM CEO Mary Barra this week about building a different vehicle at the Lordstown plant, so workers can keep their jobs. I've called for Congressional hearings on how GM used the massive tax break they received last year. But my most important mission is to make sure that GM, Congress, and the Trump Administration hear YOUR story. "" "" Disclaimer: We will only share your first name, city, and story. I remain heartbroken and furious about this situation, and the media has noticed: WKBN: "Congressman Ryan has harsh words for GM after it pulls Chevy Cruze" Washington Examiner: "Tim Ryan boils over in floor speech after GM layoffs" WKSU: "Rep. Tim Ryan Calls for Congressional Review of General Motors" GM Lordstown was an anchor for our community in Ohio's 13th District. I know across the country, these layoffs have effects that will echo far past one administration or one news cycle. I will fight tooth and nail to make sure the new Congress takes bold, immediate action to keep jobs in the United States. It's imperative that we generate more opportunities so workers and their families can earn a living wage, afford high-quality health care, send their kids to good schools, and retire with dignity. "" Thank you so much, \-- Tim P.S. If you don't have a story to share but know someone who might, "" Update Email Preferences "" "" | | Meet Tim Ryan Tim Ryan is a champion for working families in Ohio's 13th District and is known for challenging both parties to do more to rebuild the middle class. Tim believes that every hardworking family deserves a good job, a quality education for their kids, affordable healthcare, and the security of knowing they can retire with dignity. Tim was elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in 2002 and has been fighting for working people ever since. | | Tim Ryan for Congress** P.O. Box 189 Niles, Ohio 44446 "" "" Paid for by Tim Ryan for Congress

name: Jay Inslee
subject: We can't just coast
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
I'm proud of Washington voters -- but we can't relax.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7add6/2f5ea88d/7a966ca9/2cbddd89/3491814982/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&utm_campaign=freeze-3&utm_medium=ema") Friend, We're poised to make bold progress when the legislative session begins next month. But listen -- before we get to work, I need your help. You might think that we can just coast by during this session. Washington state voters elected a progressive legislature. And while I'm incredibly proud of that, we can't simply relax. Yesterday, I released my climate agenda for this session. You know just how critical it is to pass this agenda. Climate change is hurting our state now. And it is putting our kids' futures at risk. It is the defining challenge of our generation. We must confront it. But make no mistake: Big oil companies and special interests will try to stop us in the legislature so that they can protect their profits. **Plain and simple: I can't get this done unless we prove our grassroots power now. I need you to show up before the legally mandated fundraising freeze in three days and prove our grassroots strength.** [**Let's make sure we head into session swinging -- help us prove our strength by being one of 2,000 donors before the state-mandated fundraising freeze at midnight on Friday!**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7add6/2f5ea88d/7a966ca9/2cbddd89/3491814982/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&utm_campaign=freeze-3&utm_medium=ema") [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7add6/2f5ea88d/7a966ca9/2cbddd89/3491814982/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&utm_campaign=freeze-3&utm_medium=ema") **Here's our plan for fighting climate change:**
* **We will slash carbon pollution across all sectors -- in electricity, transportation, buildings, and industry.**
* **We will achieve 100% clean energy and build a clean energy economy and the good-paying jobs that come with it.**
* **Our plan requires power utilities to be carbon-neutral by 2030, and 100% clean energy by 2045.**
* **It implements new rules for zero-emission vehicles and clean fuels. And it requires more energy-efficient buildings, eliminates climate super-polluting hydrofluorocarbons, and it invests in clean energy infrastructure.**

We can't let our resources slip while we're working towards something this important. Big oil special interests are going to hate this, and that's exactly why we have to work together to get this done. [**Don't let them halt our climate progress. Help us reach our goal to be 2,000 donations strong before the freeze starts on December 14th!**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7add6/2f5ea88d/7a966ca9/2cbddd89/3491814982/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&utm_campaign=freeze-3&utm_medium=ema") Let's put in the work -- so we can see some results. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7add6/2f5ea88d/7a966ca9/2cbddd89/3491814982/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ji-fr-freeze-1214?utm_source=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&refcode=ema_2018-12-11_freeze-3&utm_campaign=freeze-3&utm_medium=ema")

subject: Chuck Schumer Says Indiana Doesn't Matter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Indiana Republican Party
body:
Yes, you read that right. Earlier today in an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump, Senate Democrat Leader Chuck Schumer suggested that... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/chuck-schumer-says-indiana-doesnt-matter?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| **Chuck Schumer: Indiana Doesn't Matter**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=64e98f3478&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **Watch As Senate Democrat Leader Laughs Off Indiana Republican U.S. Senate Victory**
Yes, you read that right. Earlier today in an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump, Senate Democrat Leader Chuck Schumer suggested that Indiana doesn't matter.

**In a[back-and-forth](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=78f7bbe682&e=1acd9bb3d6) on border security and funding for a border wall (jump to 11:35 in [linked video](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=65003d2c03&e=1acd9bb3d6)), President Trump highlighted that Republicans gained seats in the U.S. Senate this year -- which shows support for his policies. **

In response, Schumer said, "When the president brags that he won North Dakota and Indiana, he’s in real trouble," simply laughing off Hoosiers as irrelevant. You really need to [watch the video](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e9fff9a008&e=1acd9bb3d6) for yourself to believe it.

**It's just another example of what we see all the time. When it comes election time, Democrats like to pretend that they care about Hoosiers. But then they go to Washington and treat us like the punchline of their joke, just like Chuck Schumer did today.**

Democrats like Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer don't care about Indiana. That's because they're too busy working to obstruct President Trump and the real progress we're seeing in Washington on major issues like border security.

**Thankfully, Hoosiers have Senator Todd Young, soon-to-be-Senator Mike Braun, and a strong Indiana Republican House delegation working hard for us, and we know that they'll always be in our corner.**

---
